name: py_exec
description: Run Python code from an inline string or from a script file. Replaces ad-hoc 'python3 -c' calls and 'cat > /tmp/*.py && python3' patterns with a single script call.
when_to_use: Use when you need to execute Python code inline to test behavior, check types, or run small experiments. Replaces inline python3 -c commands and the common pattern of writing a .py file to /tmp and then running it.

parameters.name: code_or_flag
parameters.description: Either inline Python code as a string, or '-f' to run a script file.

parameters.name: script_file
parameters.description: When using '-f', the path to the Python script file to execute.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Additional arguments passed to the script when using '-f'.

examples.call: main.sh 'import sys; sys.path.insert(0, "/app"); from scapy.all import *; print("OK")'

examples.expected: Output of executing the script file.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-4 separate bash commands (cat writing a script to /tmp, then python3 running it) with a single script call. For inline code, replaces python3 -c calls while keeping the same single-step efficiency. Reduces token usage by avoiding file I/O boilerplate.
